questions.prompt_html:
A tank contains 200 litres of fluid in which 60 grams of salt is dissolved. A brine solution of 2 grams of salt per litre is pumped into the tank at a rate of 2 litres/minute. The well-stirred solution is then pumped out at the same rate.
(a)Write the differential equation in terms of Q(t) and t and write the initial condition to model this 
problem. (Q(t) should represent the amount of salt in the tank at time t).
(b)Solve the equation in part (a).
(c)How much salt will be present in the tank after a long period of time?
questions.prompt_text: A tank contains 200 litres of fluid in which 60 grams of salt is dissolved. A brine solution of 2 grams of salt per litre is pumped into the tank at a rate of 2 litres/minute. The well-stirred solution is then pumped out at the same rate. (a) Write the differential equation in terms of Q(t) and t and write the initial condition to model this problem. (Q(t) should represent the amount of salt in the tank at time t). (b) Solve the equation in part (a). (c) How much salt will be present in the tank after a long period of time?

choices.label: C

questions.prompt_html: The 2-manifold M in R4 given by the equations 2x1 + x4 = 0, x2 - 5x3 = 0, 0 < x2 < 1, 0 < x4 < 1 has normals n1 = 2e1+ e4, n2 = e2 - 5 e3. It is oriented by the 2-form ω(x)(v1, v2) = det(n1 n2 v1 v2). Let x = (x1, x2, x3, x4) = P(u) = (u1, -5u2, -u2, - 2u1) be a parametrization for M. Which of the following statements is true?
questions.prompt_text: The 2-manifold M in R 4 given by the equations 2x 1 + x 4 = 0, x 2 - 5x 3 = 0, 0 < x 2 < 1, 0 < x 4 < 1 has normals n 1 = 2 e 1 + e 4 , n 2 = e 2 - 5 e 3 . It is oriented by the 2-form ω( x )( v 1 , v 2 ) = det( n 1 n 2 v 1 v 2 ). Let x = (x 1 , x 2 , x 3 , x 4 ) = P (u) = (u 1 , -5u 2 , -u 2 , - 2u 1 ) be a parametrization for M. Which of the following statements is true?

choices.label: A
choices.html: P is orientation reversing for M, but no orientation preserving parametrization for M exists.
choices.text: P is orientation reversing for M, but no orientation preserving parametrization for M exists.

choices.label: B
choices.html: P is orientation preserving for M.
choices.text: P is orientation preserving for M.

choices.label: C
choices.html: P is orientation reversing for M, but q(u) = (u1, 5u2, u2, -2u1) would be orientation preserving parametrization for M.
choices.text: P is orientation reversing for M, but q (u) = (u 1 , 5u 2 , u 2 , -2u 1 ) would be orientation preserving parametrization for M.

choices.label: D
choices.html: P does not determine an orientation for M.
choices.text: P does not determine an orientation for M.

choices.label: E
choices.html: P is orientation reversing for M, but q(u) = (-u1, 5u2, u2, 2u1) would be an orientation-preserving parametrization for M.
choices.text: P is orientation reversing for M, but q (u) = (-u 1 , 5u 2 , u 2 , 2u 1 ) would be an orientation-preserving parametrization for M.

questions.prompt_html:
A tank contains 200 litres of fluid in which 60 grams of salt is dissolved. A brine solution of 2 grams of salt per litre is pumped into the tank at a rate of 2 litres/minute. The well-stirred solution is then pumped out at the same rate.
(a) Write the differential equation in terms of Q(t) and t and write the initial condition to model this problem. (Q(t) should represent the amount of salt in the tank at time t.)
(b) Solve the equation in part (a).
(c) How much salt will be present in the tank after a long period of time?

questions.prompt_text: Find the function y = y(x) where y(a) = y 1 , y(b) = y 2 that is positive on the interval [a,b] and that, when its graph is rotated about the y-axis, generates a surface of revolution of minimum area.

choices.label: A

y = 2c + d, for some constant real numbers c, d. to be chosen to satisfy boundary 
conditions provided

conditions provided

choices.label: E

y = c sinh-1(x/c) + d, for some constant real numbers c, d. to be chosen to satisfy boundary

questions.prompt_html:
Among all continuously differentiable curves y = y(x) ≥ 0, x∈ [x1, x2], find equations of the curve joining the points P (x1, 0), Q (x2,0), its length L > x2 - x1, and the area enclosed by the curve and the line segment PQ is maximum. 
Refer to the figure below.
questions.prompt_text: Among all continuously differentiable curves y = y(x) ≥ 0, x∈ [x 1 , x 2 ], find equations of the curve joining the points P (x 1 , 0), Q (x 2 ,0), its length L > x 2 - x 1 , and the area enclosed by the curve and the line segment PQ is maximum. Refer to the figure below. Short Description: A graph shows a concave down curve in the first quadrant. The curve slopes upward from point P on the x axis, goes to a maximum value, and then slopes downward to point Q on the horizontal axis.

choices.label: A
choices.html: Part of the ellipse (x -a)<sup>2</sup> + 4 (y-b)<sup>2 </sup>= λ<sup>2</sup> above the x-axis, where a, b, and λ are constant real numbers to be determined by boundary conditions provided.
choices.text: Part of the ellipse (x -a) 2 + 4 (y-b) 2 = λ 2 above the x-axis, where a, b, and λ are constant real numbers to be determined by boundary conditions provided.

choices.label: B
choices.html: Part of the parabola y = a (x-b)<sup>2</sup>+ λ above the x-axis, where a, b, and λ are constant real numbers to be determined by boundary conditions provided.
choices.text: Part of the parabola y = a (x-b) 2 + λ above the x-axis, where a, b, and λ are constant real numbers to be determined by boundary conditions provided.

choices.label: C
choices.html: Part of the circle (x -a)<sup>2</sup> + (y-b)<sup>2 </sup>= λ<sup>2</sup> above the x-axis, where a, b, and λ are constant real numbers to be determined by boundary conditions provided.
choices.text: Part of the circle (x -a) 2 + (y-b) 2 = λ 2 above the x-axis, where a, b, and λ are constant real numbers to be determined by boundary conditions provided.

choices.label: D
choices.html: Part of the ellipse 4(x -a)<sup>2</sup> + (y-b)<sup>2 </sup>= λ<sup>2</sup> above the x-axis, where a, b, and λ are constant real numbers to be determined by boundary conditions provided.
choices.text: Part of the ellipse 4(x -a) 2 + (y-b) 2 = λ 2 above the x-axis, where a, b, and λ are constant real numbers to be determined by boundary conditions provided.

choices.label: E
choices.html: Part of the hyperbola (x -a)<sup>2</sup> - (y-b)<sup>2 </sup>= - λ<sup>2</sup> above the x-axis, where a, b, and λ are constant real numbers to be determined by boundary conditions provided.
choices.text: Part of the hyperbola (x -a) 2 - (y-b) 2 = - λ 2 above the x-axis, where a, b, and λ are constant real numbers to be determined by boundary conditions provided.

Solve the problem.

An investment of P dollars is deposited in a savings account that is compounded quarterly with an annual interest rate of r, where r is expressed as a decimal. The amount of money A in the account after t years is given by A = P (1 + r/4)4t. Use this equation to determine the time it takes for an investment of $10,000 to increase to $12,000 if it is placed in an account that is compounded quarterly with an annual interest rate of 1.6%. Round to the nearest tenth.

questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. The spread of a cold virus can be modeled using logistic equations. Solve the problem.

A 500 gallon tank is filled with pure water. A salt solution with a concentration of 11 g/gal flows into the tank at a rate of 7 gal/min. The thoroughly mixed solution is drained from the tank at a rate of 7 gal/min.
(a) Write an initial value problem for the mass of the salt.
(b) Solve the initial value problem.

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

Consider the pair of differential equations that model a predator-prey system with populations x and y.
 x'(t) = 3x - 6xy
 y'(t) = -y + 7xy
(a) Find the lines along which x'(t) = 0,
(b) find the lines along which y'(t) = 0, 
(c) find the equilibrium points for the system.

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

According to a country's census, the population (to the nearest million) was 266 in Year 0 and 306 in Year 10. The projected population for Year 50 is 445. To construct a logistic model, both the growth and carrying capacity must be estimated. 
(a) Estimate r by assuming that t = 0 corresponds to Year 0 and that the population between Year 0 and Year 10 is exponential; that is, the population is given by  Round the value of r to four decimal places, if necessary.
(b) Write the solution to the logistic equation using the estimated value of r and use the projected value P(50) = 445 million to find an estimation for the value of the carrying capacity K. Round to the nearest million.
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. According to a country's census, the population (to the nearest million) was 266 in Year 0 and 306 in Year 10. The projected population for Year 50 is 445. To construct a logistic model, both the growth and carrying capacity must be estimated. (a) Estimate r by assuming that t = 0 corresponds to Year 0 and that the population between Year 0 and Year 10 is exponential; that is, the population is given by P(t) = P(0)(e) with superscript (rt). Round the value of r to four decimal places, if necessary. (b) Write the solution to the logistic equation using the estimated value of r and use the projected value P(50) = 445 million to find an estimation for the value of the carrying capacity K. Round to the nearest million.

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

A simple electrical circuit consists of a resistor connected between the terminals of a battery. The voltage V (in volts) is dropping as the battery wears out. At the same time, the resistance R (in ohms) is increasing as the resistor heats up. The power P (in watts) dissipated by the circuit is given by P =  . Use the equation
 =   +  
to find how much the power is changing at the instant when R = 2 ohms, V = 2 volts,  and dV/dt = -0.04 volts/sec.
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. A simple electrical circuit consists of a resistor connected between the terminals of a battery. The voltage V (in volts) is dropping as the battery wears out. At the same time, the resistance R (in ohms) is increasing as the resistor heats up. The power P (in watts) dissipated by the circuit is given by P = ((V) with superscript (2)/R) . Use the equation (dP/dt) = (∂P/∂V) (dV/dt) + (∂P/∂R) (dR/dt) to find how much the power is changing at the instant when R = 2 ohms, V = 2 volts, dR/dt = 0.05 ohms/sec and dV/dt = -0.04 volts/sec.

questions.prompt_html:
Write Converse and Contrapositive
Write the converse and contrapositive.
questions.prompt_text: 1) If it rains today, then it will snow tomorrow. A) Converse: If it snows tomorrow, then it will rain today. Contrapositive: If it does not snow tomorrow, then it will not rain today. B) Converse: If it snows tomorrow, then it will rain today. Contrapositive: If it does not rain today, then it will not snow tomorrow. C) Converse: If it does not snow tomorrow, then it will not rain today. Contrapositive: If it snows tomorrow, then it will rain today. D) Converse: If it snows tomorrow, then it will not rain today. Contrapositive: If it does not snow tomorrow, then it will rain today.

Write the converse and contrapositive.
questions.prompt_text: 2) If angle ABC is obtuse, then its measure is greater than 90° and less than 180° . A) Converse: If the measure of angle ABC is greater than 90° and less than 180°, then it is obtuse. Contrapositive: If the measure of angle ABC is not greater than 90° and less than 180°, then it is not obtuse. B) Converse: If the measure of angle ABC is not greater than 90° and less than 180°, then it is not obtuse. Contrapositive: If the measure of angle ABC is greater than 90° and less than 180°, then it is obtuse. C) Converse: If the measure of angle ABC is greater than 90° and less than 180°, then it is obtuse. Contrapositive: If angle ABC is not obtuse, then its measure is not greater than 90° and less than 180° . D) Converse: If angle ABC is not obtuse, then its measure is not greater than 90° and less than 180° . Contrapositive: If the measure of angle ABC is not greater than 90° and less than 180°, then it is not obtuse.
